steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This Text Structure and Purpose question asks for the overall structure of the passage. The decisive clue is the final sentence: researchers "are hopeful that this discovery could aid in the development of new antibiotics for human use" — the passage pivots from what was found to where it points next, signaling a two-part structure: findings, then future research direction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It summarizes research findings on Matabele ants and then identifies an area for further research.: Matches the passage exactly. The first two sentences report what the team discovered (ants detect and treat infected wounds; mortality reduced by 90%). The final sentence points toward future antibiotic development. ✓
  • Choice B — It introduces a study of Matabele ants and then explains the research methods used in the study.: The passage never describes how the researchers conducted their work — no methods, procedures, or experimental design are mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — It describes unique properties of Matabele ants and then speculates on how those properties evolved.: The passage does not address evolution or how the ants' wound-treatment ability developed over time. ✗
  • Choice D — It identifies an issue concerning Matabele ants and then proposes a solution to address the issue.: The ants' wound-treatment behavior is presented as a beneficial discovery, not as a problem. No issue affecting the ants is raised and no solution is proposed. ✗
A is correct.
